diagrams.description: Labor economics analyzes the functioning and dynamics of labor markets. The flowchart illustrates how the field’s core objective – to understand how wages and employment are determined – is achieved by studying three key components: labor supply (workers’ decisions on participation, hours, and human capital), labor demand (firms’ hiring based on marginal productivity and costs), and labor market institutions (unions, minimum wage, unemployment insurance). These components interact to determine observable outcomes such as wages, employment levels, and inequality. The outcomes, in turn, may feed back into the design of labor market institutions and policies, creating a continuous cycle of analysis and intervention.
